project.name: sc101-lab-interface
project.summary: Interactive learning platform with live terminal via LXD
project.description:
A KillerCoda-style interactive learning platform that provides
markdown-based tutorials with live terminal sessions powered by LXD containers.
Features include multi-user sessions, teacher-student terminal sharing,
and real-time collaboration.

build.override_build_extra:
# Install Node.js 20.x via NodeSource
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
apt-get install -y nodejs

# Build frontend
cd frontend
npm ci
npm run build
cd ..

# Install backend dependencies
cd backend
npm ci --omit=dev
cd ..

# Copy built files to install location
cp -r backend $CRAFT_PART_INSTALL/
cp -r frontend/dist $CRAFT_PART_INSTALL/frontend
cp -r tutorials $CRAFT_PART_INSTALL/
cp start.sh $CRAFT_PART_INSTALL/
chmod +x $CRAFT_PART_INSTALL/start.sh

interfaces.name: network
interfaces.reason: Required for WebSocket connections and API requests

interfaces.name: network-bind
interfaces.reason: Backend server listens on port 3001, frontend dev server on 5173

interfaces.name: lxd
interfaces.reason: Required to manage LXD containers for terminal sessions

notes: The application requires the LXD snap to be installed and initialized on the host system
The 'lxd' interface must be manually connected after installation: sudo snap connect sc101-lab-interface:lxd lxd:lxd
Frontend is pre-built during snap creation and served as static files
The start.sh script manages both backend and frontend processes as a single daemon
